statement.title: Statement
statement.content:
Input Format
The only line of input contains three integers $ x $ , $ y $ , $ z $ ( $ -1000 \le x, y, z \le 1000 $ ).

Output Format
Output one real number — the answer.

Your answer is considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed $ 10^6 $ . Formally, let your answer be $ a $ , and the jury's answer be $ b $ . Your answer is accepted if and only if $ \frac{|a-b|}{\max(1,|b|)} \le 10^6 $ .

Examples
Sample 1
Input
1 1 1
Output
1.7320508075688772

Sample 2
Input
1 -2 -2
Output
21.727922061357855

Sample 3
Input
998 244 353
Output
-0.0006269592476489029

Sample 4
Input
0 0 0
Output
1792283455048516.3

solutions.title: Solution
solutions.content:
1812E - Not a Geometry Problem
The error should be at most $$$10^{+6}$$$, instead of the usual $$$10^{-6}$$$.
This means that you can just output $$$0$$$, since then $$$\frac{|a-b|}{\max(1,|b|)} = \frac{|-b|}{\max(1,|b|)} = \frac{|b|}{\max(1,|b|)} \le 10^6$$$.
